Position in beads.jsonl\n        self._processed_beads: set[str] = set()\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Start the bead watcher daemon.\"\"\"\n        self._running = True\n        self._task = asyncio.create_task(self._watch_loop())\n        logger.info(\"Bead watcher started\")\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Stop the bead watcher daemon.\"\"\"\n        self._running = False\n        if self._task:\n            self._task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await self._task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n        logger.info(\"Bead watcher stopped\")\n\n    async def _watch_loop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Main watch loop - checks for new bead events.\"\"\"\n        while self._running:\n            try:\n                await self._check_for_events()\n                await asyncio.sleep(self.CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS)\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                break\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error in bead watch loop: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n                await asyncio.sleep(self.CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS)\n\n    async def _check_for_events(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Check for new bead events and process them.\"\"\"\n        beads_path = Path(self.BEADS_JSONL)\n        if not beads_path.exists():\n            return\n\n        # Seek to last position\n                f.seek(self._last_position)\n                new_lines = f.readlines()\n\n                # Update position\n                self._last_position = f.tell()\n\n                for line in new_lines:\n                    if not line.strip():\n                        continue\n\n                    try:\n                        bead = json.loads(line)\n                        bead_id = bead.get(\"id\")\n                        status = bead.get(\"status\")\n\n                        if not bead_id or bead_id in self._processed_beads:\n                            continue\n\n                        # Process closed beads\n                        if status == \"closed\":\n                            events.append(BeadEvent(\n                                bead_id=bead_id,\n                                event_type=\"closed\",\n                                timestamp=int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n                                data=bead,\n                            ))\n                            self._processed_beads.add(bead_id)\n\n                    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n                        logger.warning(f\"Failed to parse bead JSON: {e}\")\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Error reading beads file: {e}\")\n\n        return events\n\n    async def _process_bead_event(self, event: BeadEvent) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Process a bead event and route to surfaces.\"\"\"\n        bead_data = event.data\n\n        # Extract metadata\n        metadata = bead_data.get(\"metadata\", {})\n        session_id = metadata.get(\"session_id\")\n        surface_id = metadata.get(\"origin_surface_id\")\n\n        if not session_id:\n            # No session_id - can't route\n            logger.warning(f\"Bead {event.bead_id} has no session_id in metadata\")\n            return\n\n        # Determine target surface\n        decision = await self.router.route_result(\n            session_id=session_id,\n            origin_surface_id=surface_id,\n            urgency=metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n        )\n\n        # Extract result from bead\n        result = await self._extract_result_from_bead(bead_data, session_id)\n\n        if not result:\n            logger.warning(f\"Could not extract result from bead {event.bead_id}\")\n            return\n\n        # Route to target surfaces\n        if decision.target_surfaces:\n            for surface in decision.target_surfaces:\n                if surface.type == \"telegram\":\n                    await self._send_to_telegram(result, session_id)\n                else:\n                    # Fallback to Telegram\n            await self._send_to_telegram(result, session_id)\n        else:\n            # No surface available - result stays in queue\n            logger.info(f\"No surface available for bead {event.bead_id}, result queued\")\n\n    async def _extract_result_from_bead(self, bead: dict, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Extract result data from a closed bead.\"\"\"\n        # Try to extract structured result from bead body\n        body = bead.get(\"body\", \"\")\n        metadata = bead.get(\"metadata\", {})\n\n        # If metadata already has result structure, use it\n        if \"result\" in metadata:\n            return metadata[\"result\"]\n\n        # Try to parse result from body\n        # For now, create a basic result from the bead\n        return {\n            \"id\": bead.get(\"id\"),\n            \"type\": \"bead_result\",\n            \"summary\": body[:200] if len(body) > 200 else body,  # Truncate for summary\n            \"data\": {\n                \"bead_id\": bead.get(\"id\"),\n                \"title\": bead.get(\"title\"),\n                \"type\": bead.get(\"type\"),\n                \"body\": body,\n                \"status\": bead.get(\"status\"),\n            },\n            \"urgency\": metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n            \"surfaced_at\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n        }\n\n    async def _send_to_telegram(self, result: dict, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Send result to Telegram via telegram-claude-bridge.\n\n        NOTE: This requires a session→telegram_chat_id mapping. Current implementation\n        logs a warning because telegram-claude-bridge uses a pull-based architecture\n        (manages sessions internally per forum topic) rather than push-based message delivery.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # telegram-claude-bridge proxy expects actual Telegram chat_id (int64), not session_id\n            # Since we don't have a session→chat mapping, log this as unavailable\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"Cannot send result to Telegram for session {session_id}: \"\n                f\"session→telegram_chat mapping not implemented. \"\n                f\"telegram-claude-bridge uses pull-based architecture (per forum topic sessions).\"\n            )\n\n            # Correct contract for reference (if mapping is implemented later):\n            # POST http://telegram-claude-bridge:8000/send\n            # {\n            #   \"chat_id\": 123456789,  # int64, REQUIRED - actual Telegram chat ID\n            #   \"text\": \"message\",     # string, REQUIRED - message content\n            #   \"parse_mode\": \"HTML\"   # string, OPTIONAL\n            # }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Error in Telegram send logic: {e}\")\n\n    def _format_telegram_message(self, result: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Format result as Telegram message.\"\"\"\n        summary = result.get(\"summary\", \"Result available\")\n        urgency = result.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\")\n\n        emoji_map = {\n            \"critical\": \"🚨\",\n            \"high\": \"⚠️\",\n            \"normal\": \"📌\",\n            \"low\": \"💬\",\n        }\n\n        emoji = emoji_map.get(urgency, \"📌\")\n\n        # Build message\n        lines = [\n            f\"{emoji} {summary}\",\n        ]\n\n        # Add detail if available\n        data = result.get(\"data\", {})\n        if \"bead_id\" in data:\n            lines.append(f\"Bead: {data['bead_id']}\")\n\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\nasync def create_bead_watcher(store: SessionStore, router: SurfaceRouter) -> BeadWatcher:\n    \"\"\"Create and start a bead watcher daemon.\"\"\"\n    watcher = BeadWatcher(store, router)\n    await watcher.start()\n    return watcher\n","numLines":274,"startLine":1,"totalLines":274}}}

Telegram Send Locations and Error Handling\n\n## Summary\n\nThis document identifies all locations in the aide-de-camp codebase where Telegram send attempts occur and documents their current error handling patterns.\n\n## Key Finding\n\n**The primary Telegram send method (`send_message` in `src/telegram/fallback.py`) is never actually called with real messages.** All calls to Telegram send are currently stubs that log warnings and return `False` due to missing session→telegram_chat_id mapping.\n\n## Location 1: `src/telegram/fallback.py` - Primary Send Implementation\n\n### Method: `send_message()`\n\n**File:** `src/telegram/fallback.py` (lines 44-93)\n\n**Signature:**\n```python\nasync def send_message(\n    self,\n    chat_id: int | str,\n    message: str,\n    parse_mode: str = \"HTML\",\n) -> bool:\n```\n\n**Purpose:** Send a message to a Telegram chat via telegram-claude-bridge proxy.\n\n**Error Handling:**\n- Catches `httpx.RequestError` for network/connection issues\n- Catches generic `Exception` for all other errors\n- Calls `_handle_send_failure()` on failure (see below)\n- Logs at `DEBUG` level for failures\n- Returns `bool` success indicator\n\n**Failure Information Available:**\n- `httpx.RequestError` exception includes connection details\n- HTTP response status code and text\n- Raw exception message\n\n**Current Behavior:**\n- Success (200): Logs at INFO, updates `_is_reachable = True`, returns `True`\n- Non-200 status: Logs at DEBUG with status code and response text\n- Request error: Logs at DEBUG with exception message\n\n**Rate-Limited Warning Logging:**\nThe `_handle_send_failure()` method (lines 197-212) implements intelligent warning logging:\n- Only logs WARNING on first failure or if >60 seconds since last logged failure\n- Subsequent failures logged at DEBUG only (prevents log spam)\n- Tracks `_failure_count` and `_last_failure_logged` timestamp\n\n### Method: `send_result()`\n\n**File:** `src/telegram/fallback.py` (lines 95-106)\n\n**Purpose:** Send structured result to Telegram.\n\n**Current Behavior:**\n- Formats result via `_format_result_message()`\n- Delegates to `send_message()`\n- Inherently has same error handling\n\n### Method: `send_exception()`\n\n**File:** `src/telegram/fallback.py` (lines 108-125)\n\n**Purpose:** Send exception to Telegram for human attention.\n\n**Current Behavior:**\n- **NOT IMPLEMENTED** - Logs WARNING and returns `False`\n- Warning: \"session→telegram_chat mapping not implemented\"\n\n### Method: `send_workload_summary()`\n\n**File:** `src/telegram/fallback.py` (lines 127-144)\n\n**Purpose:** Send workload summary to Telegram.\n\n**Current Behavior:**\n- **NOT IMPLEMENTED** - Logs WARNING and returns `False`\n- Warning: \"session→telegram_chat mapping not implemented\"\n\n## Location 2: `src/watcher/daemon.py` - Bead Watcher Telegram Send\n\n### Method: `_send_to_telegram()`\n\n**File:** `src/watcher/daemon.py` (lines 215-241)\n\n**Purpose:** Send result to Telegram when bead is closed and target surface is Telegram.\n\n**Error Handling:**\n- Catches generic `Exception` \n- Logs at ERROR level\n- No return value (void)\n\n**Failure Information Available:**\n- Generic exception message\n\n**Current Behavior:**\n- **NOT IMPLEMENTED** - Logs WARNING and exits\n- Warning explains: \"session→telegram_chat mapping not implemented. telegram-claude-bridge uses pull-based architecture (per forum topic sessions).\"\n- Includes reference contract comment for future implementation\n\n**Call Chain:**\n1. `_process_bead_event()` → line 172 (when surface.type == \"telegram\")\n2. `_process_bead_event()` → line 182 (when fallback_used is True)\n\n### Helper Method: `_format_telegram_message()`\n\n**File:** `src/watcher/daemon.py` (lines 242-266)\n\n**Purpose:** Format result as Telegram message.\n\n**Current Behavior:**\n- Constructs message with urgency emoji\n- Includes bead_id from result data\n- Not currently called (send is stubbed)\n\n## Location 3: `src/main.py` - Startup Bridge Check\n\n### Method: `startup()` - Bridge Reachability Check\n\n**File:** `src/main.py` (startup event handler)\n\n**Purpose:** Check if telegram-claude-bridge is reachable at startup.\n\n**Error Handling:**\n- Catches generic `Exception`\n- Logs at WARNING level\n\n**Current Behavior:**\n- Calls `telegram_fallback.check_bridge_available()`\n- Logs INFO if reachable\n- Logs WARNING if unreachable\n- Logs WARNING if check fails\n\n### API Endpoint: `api_v1_telegram_bridge_status()`\n\n**File:** `src/main.py` (endpoint handler)\n\n**Purpose:** Expose bridge status via REST API.\n\n**Error Handling:**\n- Catches generic `Exception`\n- Returns 500 error with message\n- Logs at ERROR level\n\n## Error Handling Pattern Summary\n\n### Logging Levels by Error Type\n\n| Error Type | Level | Location |\n|------------|-------|----------|\n| First bridge failure in batch | WARNING | `fallback.py:207` |\n| Subsequent failures (<60s) | DEBUG | `fallback.py:82,88,92` |\n| Request error (network) | DEBUG | `fallback.py:88` |\n| Generic send error | DEBUG | `fallback.py:92` |\n| Daemon Telegram send error | ERROR | `daemon.py:240` |\n| Startup check failure | WARNING | `main.py:startup` |\n| Status API error | ERROR | `main.py:api_v1_telegram_bridge_status` |\n| Unimplemented send methods | WARNING | `fallback.py:120,139` |\n\n### State Tracking\n\n**`TelegramFallback` instance maintains:**\n- `_is_reachable`: bool or None (None=unknown, True=reachable,
